response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned full-stack and systems developer with broad experience, but fails multiple hard requirements for this role: no Japanese language proficiency (native-level is required), no demonstrated Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and no background in the core security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 switch development. C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments a decade ago, far short of the strong UNIX C proficiency required for embedded LAN-switch firmware work.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded contexts
C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and low-level systems work (audio server, high-performance DLang services)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling, and open-source contributions
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a hard requirement for the role
No experience with network security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, MAC auth) or L2/L3 switching
No Linux kernel analysis/modification experience; recent work is web/full-stack, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: Long general software engineering track record
